context: You are an experienced product manager reviewing the Autonomous SME Control Tower — an AI-powered operations platform targeting small and medium enterprises across Africa (Nigeria primary, expanding to Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, Rwanda, UK). The platform is NOT accounting software. It is positioned as an AI business survival and growth assistant that helps SME owners who may have no accounting knowledge make sense of their business data and take autonomous action.

platform_summary.name: Autonomous SME Control Tower
platform_summary.tagline: AI-powered business survival and growth assistant for African SMEs
platform_summary.origin: Built as a Nova-first agentic system for AWS Bedrock hackathon, now evolving to commercially-ready Nigerian SME platform

tech_stack.backend: FastAPI + Python, Pydantic models, 30 API routers, 22 AI agents
tech_stack.frontend: Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS, 25+ pages, collapsible tier-aware sidebar
tech_stack.ai_models: AWS Bedrock Nova (Lite for text, Embeddings for semantic search, Act for agentic actions, Sonic for voice)
tech_stack.storage: DynamoDB (16 tables, all keyed by org_id), S3 (document storage)
tech_stack.deployment: Docker-first local dev, Vercel (frontend), backend TBD

target_market.secondary: East Africa informal and semi-formal SMEs
user_personas: SME owner with no accounting knowledge who needs actionable insights
Multi-branch retail operator needing unified dashboards
Informal trader using WhatsApp and handwritten receipts
Growing business transitioning from manual to digital operations

steps.name: Ingest
steps.detail: Collect business data from invoices (PDF/image), emails, POS exports, receipts, camera captures, WhatsApp messages, CSV/Excel, manual entry

steps.name: Diagnose
steps.detail: Analyse signals, calculate Nova Stability Index (NSI) across liquidity, revenue stability, operational latency, vendor risk

steps.name: Simulate
steps.detail: Generate AI strategy options with predicted NSI improvements, cost estimates, automation eligibility

steps.name: Execute
steps.detail: Take autonomous actions via Nova Act with human-in-the-loop oversight (WhatsApp approval for high-priority)

steps.name: Evaluate
steps.detail: Measure prediction accuracy, compare actual vs predicted NSI changes, refine approach

authentication.registration: Multi-step with email OTP verification (SHA-256 hashed, 10-min expiry)
authentication.rbac: 5-level hierarchy: viewer < member < admin < owner < super_admin
authentication.team_management: Invite users, assign roles, remove members

data_ingestion.manual_upload: CSV/Excel with AI column mapping (confidence scores)
data_ingestion.email_ingestion: Email parsing with task extraction, triggers WhatsApp agent for human-in-the-loop
data_ingestion.whatsapp: Message extraction, business summaries, action review/approval

ai_agents: Tax (multi-country compliance: NG, GH, KE, ZA, RW, GB)
frontend_pages: Landing page (Africa-focused with business type showcase)
Registration (multi-step with country flag phone input)
Transactions (list, filters, summary, overdue payment tracking)
Finance (document management, P&L, cashflow, analytics, export)
Voice Assistant (text mode + voice mode toggle)
POS Connector / Bank Sync / Desktop Sync
Admin Panel (super_admin: platform metrics, user management)
security: JWT auth on all /api/ routes (except public endpoints)
Org isolation middleware (JWT org_id must match request org_id)
Tiered rate limiting (120 RPM general, 10 RPM auth, 20 RPM uploads)
Input validation (org_id regex, file type/size checks)
Safe error messages (no internal details in production)
Body org_id validation on all POST endpoints

multi_country_tax.nigeria_specifics: CIT: 0% under ₦25M, 20% ₦25M-₦100M, 30% above. VAT 7.5%. WHT 5%. PAYE threshold ₦800K (Nigeria Tax Act 2025)

phase_1_nigeria_launch: Manual ingestion (CSV, Excel, PDF, image, camera)
phase_3_scale: AI forecasting engine (revenue, expense, cash runway)

success_criteria: African SMEs can onboard without accounting knowledge
Free tier drives adoption — no friction to start
Supermarkets see actionable insights within 10 minutes of onboarding
Schema supports multiple SME types without redesign
Platform feels Africa-native and practical (not a Western SaaS clone)
known_gaps_and_risks: Implementation status doc is outdated (still shows hackathon-era TODOs)
No payment integration yet (Paystack/Flutterwave for NGN subscriptions)
No mobile app — web-only, though mobile-first responsive design
SES still in sandbox mode — email sending limited to verified addresses
Backend deployment strategy not finalised (Docker on EC2/ECS vs serverless)
No real POS hardware integration yet — file-based import only
WhatsApp integration is webhook-based, not yet connected to WhatsApp Business API
Voice uses browser Web Speech API — no server-side speech processing
No offline mode for areas with poor connectivity
No localisation beyond English (Pidgin, Yoruba, Hausa, Swahili could increase adoption)
Enterprise tier has no self-serve upgrade path — 'Contact Us' only
Admin dashboard shows metrics but no billing/subscription management
No A/B testing or feature flag infrastructure
No analytics/telemetry for user behaviour tracking
Prediction accuracy feedback loop (reeval agent) not yet validated with real data

review_instructions.objective: Review the entire application from a product management perspective. Evaluate product-market fit, feature prioritisation, pricing strategy, UX flow, and go-to-market readiness for Nigerian SME launch.

questions: Does the feature set match what Nigerian SMEs actually need day-to-day?
Are we solving real pain points or building impressive-but-unused AI features?
Is the core loop (Ingest → Diagnose → Simulate → Execute → Evaluate) too complex for the target user?
Should we simplify the initial experience to focus on 2-3 killer features?
Which business types should we prioritise for the Nigeria pilot?
Is ₦14,900/mo ($9.99) realistic for Nigerian SMEs? What's the willingness to pay?
Is the free tier generous enough to drive adoption but limited enough to drive upgrades?
Should Growth tier include multi-branch (currently Business-only)?
Is the feature gating between tiers intuitive or confusing?
Should we offer annual pricing discounts?
Is 'Contact Us' for Enterprise the right approach or should we show a price?
Can a kiosk owner with a smartphone complete onboarding in under 5 minutes?
Is the sidebar navigation with 7 groups and 25+ items overwhelming?
Should we implement a guided first-use experience (wizard/tour)?
Is the dashboard information density appropriate for the target user?
Are lock icons on tier-gated features motivating or frustrating?
Should the landing page focus more on specific business type stories?
Which Phase 2/3 features should be pulled forward for Nigeria launch?
Are there features we've built that should be deprioritised or removed?
Is the AI voice assistant a differentiator or a distraction at this stage?
Should WhatsApp ingestion be Phase 1 given how central WhatsApp is to Nigerian business?
Is multi-country tax support premature or a competitive advantage?
Should we invest in Paystack/Flutterwave integration before anything else?
What's missing for a credible Nigeria soft launch?
Do we need a mobile app or is responsive web sufficient for Phase 1?
Should we partner with specific supermarket chains for the pilot?
What's the customer acquisition strategy beyond the free tier?
Do we need local language support (Pidgin, Yoruba, Hausa) for adoption?
How do we handle customer support at launch?
How do we differentiate from existing Nigerian fintech/accounting tools (Bumpa, Kippa, Prospa)?
Is 'AI business survival assistant' positioning clear and compelling?
Should we lean harder into specific verticals (e.g., supermarket-first)?
Is the Nova Stability Index (NSI) concept understandable to SME owners?
Is the 22-agent architecture over-engineered for current needs?
Should we consolidate agents to reduce complexity and maintenance burden?
Is DynamoDB the right choice or should we consider a relational DB for complex queries?
What's the cost model for Bedrock API calls at scale?
How do we handle the transition from hackathon architecture to production?

deliverables_requested: Priority-ranked list of changes needed before Nigeria soft launch
Metrics and KPIs to track post-launch
